details.calculation: ($84,147,711 / 12,897 total beneficiaries) = $6,524.60 per outcome. | A $1,000 donation achieves ≈ 0.153 outcomes.

significant_events.summary: Rescues and treats un-owned street animals of Udaipur who are ill or injured, inspiring the community to protect all animals.

significant_events.source_quote: A 120% increase compared to 2022-23, due to the reopening of our Animal Birth Control center and the launch of our dog sterilization program in January 2023, under an MoU with the local authorities.
significant_events.summary: Launched in January 2023 with local authorities, reopening the Animal Birth Control center, leading to a 120% increase in sterilizations.

significant_events.source_quote: Animals who cannot be released back in their area--the disabled, blind, old, and orphaned--live and thrive in our sanctuary permanently.
significant_events.summary: Provides permanent care for disabled, blind, old, and orphaned animals that cannot be released back into their areas.

significant_events.source_quote: The planning of our new rescue center has begun! In October 2023, we finalized the purchase of (almost) two more bighas, which provide us a strategic access to a side road and increased the total area of the new land to 8.3 hectares.
significant_events.summary: Planning for a new rescue center has begun, with land acquisition increasing total area to 8.3 hectares in October 2023.

significant_events.source_quote: It has hosted more than 500 volunteers who have spent meaningful time helping to save the lives of animals.
significant_events.summary: Spreads awareness on compassion for animals in Udaipur's schools, inspiring children to treat animals with respect and kindness.

data.original_text: Our mission is to rescue and treat owner-less street animals of Udaipur who have become ill or injured, and through their rescue inspire the community to care for the lives of all animals.
Every year we rescue more than 15,000 injured animals in Udaipur, India.
Throughout India, animals including dogs, cows, cats and donkeys live on the streets.
We offer tours everyday at 10:30AM, 12PM, 2:30PM and 3:30PM to share the stories of the animals we rescue, educate about the problems animals face in India and the different ways to help.
The charity's objective is the development of compassion and sensitivity in people of all ages, especially the young, through education about the needs of animals.

Animal Aid Unlimited or AAU, founded in 2002, is an Indian animal rescue organization, based in Udaipur city of Rajasthan which rescues and treats animals that are sick, injured, stuck or in need of urgent medical aid and attention.
As of 2017, the organization claims to have saved more than 45,000 injured or ill dogs, cows, donkeys, birds and cats.
As of January 2025, their YouTube channel has over 7.34 million subscribers.

Mission: Our mission is to rescue and treat the un-owned street animals of Udaipur who have become ill or injured, and through their rescue inspire a community to protect and defend the lives of all animals.
Target demographics: ill and injured street animals.
Direct beneficiaries per year: 15517 rescued animals.
Programs: spay and nueter, rescue and hospital, rehabilitation and sanctuary.
Over the years Animal Aid has helped hundreds of thousands of ill and injured animals to get back on their feet.

The registered charity, Animal Aid Charitable Trust (was Animal Aid (Campaign Group)) Ltd (company number 08126689) was incorporated on 2nd July 2012, (registered charity number 1152685). Our charitable objectives are as follows: 'The development of compassion and sensitivity in people of all ages, through education about the needs of animals and ways in which they are exploited and made to suffer, including (without limitation) by the provision of information and support to enable people to adopt a cruelty-free (vegan) lifestyle that promotes the protection of animals and the environment and benefits of human health. '

Animal Aid Unlimited funds and provides honorary management to the Indian charity Animal Aid Charitable Trust (AACT) to relieve animal suffering in India through street animal rescue, medical treatment, sanctuary, and compassion education.

Our helpline received 33,237 calls for rescue requests, an average of 101 calls per day. We rescued 17,723 ownerless animals from Udaipur and nearby villages. We admitted 12,897 animals into our hospital for treatment: 10,211 dogs and puppies; 1,058 cows and bulls and 1,628 donkeys, cats and other animals.
We vaccinated every animal admitted against rabies and other common diseases.
We completed 6,955 on site visits to provide medical treatment to animals in their neighbourhoods.
We spayed / neutered 4,177 animals (a 50% increase compared to 2023.
Since 1977, through our campaigning and education work, we have led the way in driving change for animals, exposing cruelty, influencing policy, and shifting hearts and minds.
Produced the UK's first anti-dairy advert – as part of our Terror on Our Tables campaign – to be shown on primetime television, including Channel 4 and Sky TV, and in cinemas nationwide.
Won the prestigious Lush Prize in 2024 for our work to end lethal dose animal tests, particularly our creative public awareness campaigning.
Landed the shooting industry with a £20 million unpaid tax bill through our detailed investigative work.
Successfully campaigned for major supermarkets – including Asda, Tesco, Aldi, Morrisons, Iceland and Waitrose – to clearly label their own-brand vegan products with our #MarkItVegan campaign.
Organised thousands of free educational talks and vegan cookery demonstrations in schools across the UK.
Launched a successful government petition to ban snares, reaching over 100,000 signatures and triggering a parliamentary debate, which helped to secure bans in both Scotland and Wales.
Exposed widespread and illegal cruelty in UK slaughterhouses, leading to the introduction of compulsory CCTV in all English slaughterhouses.
Launched a landmark exposé into the horse racing industry, which was featured on the BBC Panorama programme 'The Dark Side of Horse Racing'.
Played a central role in halting the University of Cambridge's plans to build a vast primate testing facility.
Partnered with XCellR8, the world's only vegan laboratory, to replace the outdated and inhumane lethal dose 50 (LD50) test with modern, human-relevant alternatives.

Humane Education Program has reached more than 150 of Udaipur's schools to spread awareness on compassion for animals and to inspire children to treat animals with the respect and kindness they deserve.
Animal Aid has rescued more than 30,000 injured and ill animals in Udaipur since 2002.
They have approached almost 15,000 students in Udaipur on compassion for animals.
It has hosted more than 500 volunteers who have spent meaningful time helping to save the lives of animals.
Animal Aid runs on-going rabies inoculation drives, which have accomplished the immunization of more than 13,000 street dogs against rabies.
Rescue team has saved more than 25,500 animals since 2002 with the help of local residents and animal lovers.
Animal Aid Charitable Trust (est. 2007) provides medical services and sanctuary to animals in Udaipur, Rajasthan India.

Animal Aid is a British animal rights organisation, founded in 1977 by Jean Pink. The group campaigns peacefully against the consumption of animals as food and against animal cruelty such as their use for medical research—and promotes a cruelty-free lifestyle. It also investigates and exposes animal abuse.

In 2023 we launched our Sterilization & Rabies Innoculation program through an MoU with the local government. Since 2023 we have spayed/neutered and vaccinated against rabies over 12,000 community/street dogs. In 2025 we sterilized 4,986 dogs.
Since 2002 AAU has treated 50,000 street animals including dogs, donkeys, cows and bulls, from aged to orphans.

name: Animal Aid Unlimited & Animal Aid Charitable Trust
